preface.purpose: The purpose of this textbook is to bridge the gap between high-level generative AI prompting guides and the deep engineering/mathematical foundations of LLMs. It details fine-tuning mathematics, preference loops, and low-level inference optimizations.
preface.target_audience: This book is designed for AI engineers, machine learning scientists, systems engineers specializing in LLM deployment, and students seeking a rigorous understanding of the parameters governing generative model behaviors.
preface.philosophy: We follow a pedagogical loop: establishing concrete conceptual intuition, defining formal mathematics, illustrating execution flows, mapping industrial deployment profiles, and analyzing common engineering mistakes and interview expectations.
preface.how_to_use: Read the chapters sequentially to build a continuous learning timeline. Use the inline LaTeX derivations to work through mathematical proofs. Pay close attention to the 'Student Trap' and 'Practical Engineering' boxes, as they detail common pitfalls observed in both coding interviews and production systems.

about_ascendrite: Ascendrite is an advanced learning ecosystem designed to visualize, track, and validate computer science and AI engineering skills. By structuring knowledge into high-density decentralized databases and dynamic 3D visualizers, Ascendrite enables engineers to rapidly build and verify industry-grade competencies.
